summary: Iteration 21 frontend test: Feature A (Input Strips) passes 100%. Feature B (Proactive Profile Hint) passes 3/6 sub-scenarios; 3 scenarios fail — fingerprint-reset re-fire, LOW-confidence suppression, and popout-mode safety. One regression testid not found.

integration_issues.issue: After dismissing home-studio suggestion, navigating to Matrix tab and toggling 6 new routes (in_phy_0 → out_phy_2..7), then returning to Channels, the proactive-hint does NOT reappear with a Car Audio MEDIUM suggestion. Expected: routing fingerprint changes → dismissal for the now-suggested profile is cleared → chip reappears.
integration_issues.likely_root_cause: ProactiveProfileHint.jsx:68 only deletes dismissed[det.profileId] for the CURRENTLY suggested profile. If detection now suggests 'car-audio' but dismissed map holds {home-studio:...}, the new car-audio entry is absent (so not dismissed) and chip SHOULD show. Either (a) detection still returns home-studio after the matrix change (heuristic not flipping to car-audio at 8 outputs as expected from iteration-20 fix), or (b) the tab-matrix/tab-channels testids used by the test don't match (top bar shows 'Routing Matrix' button) and the matrix toggles never executed. Recommend manual verification.

integration_issues.issue: With matrix fully cleared (default 2 routes toggled off → 0 active outputs), the proactive-hint is STILL visible. Expected: detectProfile returns LOW → component returns null.
integration_issues.likely_root_cause: Either (1) the matrix toggle clicks in the test did not actually clear default routes (tab navigation issue), or (2) detectProfile() still returns medium+ when matrix is empty contrary to iteration-20 scenario 3. Iteration-20 verified empty matrix returns LOW so most likely (1). HOWEVER, given the page screenshot of the proactive chip persists, this needs main agent verification.

integration_issues.issue: Navigating to URL with #popout=meters renders the proactive-hint (chip count > 0). Expected: component returns null when window.location.hash === '#popout=meters'.
integration_issues.likely_root_cause: Two possibilities: (a) The full DSPApp still renders for #popout=meters (popout mode may not be wired at the App.js router level to render a standalone MetersView), in which case ProactiveProfileHint.jsx:78 check is correct but fails because the component subtree still mounts (the chip is rendered AT the time we counted). Actually re-reading code: the hash check at line 78 DOES return null before the suggested-profile computation. Verify the popout URL actually has '#popout=meters' as the literal hash after navigation. (b) The popout window mode is supposed to render a different component tree entirely (not DSPShell) — if so, the existence of any proactive-hint at #popout=meters indicates DSPApp was rendered when it shouldn't be.

regressions.issue: Not found on Channels tab. Other ChannelStrip controls render normally. May be moved/renamed.

action_items: Verify Feature B fingerprint-reset: detect why chip does not re-fire with Car Audio MEDIUM after routing in_phy_0 → out_phy_2..7. Re-run heuristic detectProfile on the resulting 8-output state and ensure dismissed[car-audio] is undefined.

Verify Feature B LOW suppression: ensure detectProfile returns 'low' when matrix is empty (this was working in iteration-20 — possibly regressed or test toggle navigation failed). The chip stayed visible in the run.

Verify Feature B popout safety: confirm whether #popout=meters route mounts DSPShell + ProactiveProfileHint at all. If yes, the hash check inside ProactiveProfileHint may be running before window is fully ready or hash is normalised differently. If no, the popout route must short-circuit before DSPApp.
Locate the testid 'out-out_phy_0-pan-visual' in ChannelStrip — appears missing or renamed.
critical_code_review_comments: ProactiveProfileHint.jsx:78 — `if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.hash === '#popout=meters') return null;` runs on every render but doesn't subscribe to hashchange. If the popout opens via window.open then the initial render in the popup uses that hash — OK. But if the same component instance is re-used across hash changes, the check is stale. Recommend adding a hashchange listener for correctness.

ProactiveProfileHint.jsx:62-75 — useEffect dependency array includes `dismissed` and `lastFp`, which can cause stale closures: when `dismissed` updates via setDismissed inside the effect, the effect re-runs and the early `if (fp !== lastFp)` is false — OK but fragile. Consider using a ref for lastFp or computing fingerprint inline.

ProactiveProfileHint.jsx uses 500ms polling (line 52-57) to detect activeId changes from MetersView. This is wasteful — recommend using a localStorage 'storage' event listener, or lifting activeId into the dspStore so React subscribes naturally without polling.
InputStrip.jsx is clean, well-commented, and follows the established testid pattern. dbToPct/pctToDb taper curve is documented. No issues found.
seed_data_creation: None. Test clears localStorage and toggles matrix cells via 'route-{inputId}-{outputId}' testids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature A passes fully — no concerns there. Feature B has 3 failures clustered around the conditional-render logic in ProactiveProfileHint.jsx. The component's check ordering is: (1) popout hash bail-out at line 78, (2) detection result at 80, (3) suppression filter at 83. Failures suggest either: detection heuristic doesn't flip to LOW on empty matrix (contradicts iteration-20 verification), OR the test's tab-matrix/tab-channels testids may not be exact (top bar button 'Routing Matrix' suggests possible 'tab-routing' or 'tab-routingmatrix'). Recommend the next testing agent explicitly check the top-bar tab testids via `await page.locator('[data-testid^="tab-"]').all_inner_texts()` before re-running Feature B scenarios.
rca_of_the_issue: Most likely the 3 Feature B failures share a common root cause: tab-navigation testids ('tab-matrix','tab-channels') used by the test did not match actual top-bar testids, so the matrix-edit scenarios never actually modified state. The 'low suppression' and 'fingerprint-reset' scenarios depend on first editing the matrix. The 'popout' scenario is independent and represents a likely real bug in either the popout route wiring or the hash check inside ProactiveProfileHint.jsx. Manual verification recommended before raising as a defect.
